questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
questions.answer: The prohibition of gas-powered leaf blowers lasts from June 1 to August 31 of each year, and from January 1 to March 31 of each year. This totals 8 months of prohibition.

questions.question: What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
questions.answer: Gas-powered leaf blowers are permitted to be operated during the following hours: from 10:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. Sunday through Saturday, and 9:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. on Saturdays. However, they cannot be operated from 6:00 p.m. Sunday until 8:00 a.m. Monday, 8:00 p.m. to 8:00 a.m. Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 p.m. Friday until 10:00 a.m. Saturday.

questions.question: What are the penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations?
questions.answer: Any person violating the gas leaf blower regulations shall be punished according to § 1-12 of Chapter 1, General Provisions. Each day that a violation continues is considered a separate offense, meaning multiple penalties can be incurred over time.

questions.question: Are there any exceptions for school/government/commercial properties?
questions.answer: Yes, there are exceptions. Sounds created by public utilities, governmental agencies, railroad agencies, and organized sporting events on public school property are exempt from the noise limitations. Additionally, the use of gas-powered leaf blowers on school district property is permitted at any time.

questions.question: How are violations reported and enforced?
questions.answer: Violations of the noise regulations, including gas leaf blower regulations, are enforced by the Police Department of the Village of Croton-on-Hudson, Code Enforcement Officers, or other officials designated by the Village Manager.

questions.question: Does the ordinance mention the benefits of leaving leaf debris and/or leaf mulching?
Does the ordinance require a report to be presented to the public?
